component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static control bar in normal flow; it appears after the list content and scrolls off the screen with the rest of the page, with no sticky/fixed behavior.

name: Equalization Tank Sizing Calculator
title: Equalization Tank Sizing Calculator
description: This page lets users input flow and process parameters to calculate a required equalization tank volume, review calculated recommendations, select a preferred configuration, and navigate to compatible equalization tank products with appropriate filters applied.

core_features: Display site header context (quote list, comparison, reading list, service inquiry counts) using the getSiteHeaderContext interface.
Provide an input form for equalization tank sizing with fields for flow rate value, flow rate unit, peak factor, and buffer/detention time, with validation and URL-parameter synchronization.
Call runEqualizationTankCalculation with the user’s inputs to obtain an EqualizationTankCalculation record and a list of recommended configurations.
Show a calculation summary including the required tank volume and the key input parameters that produced it.
List one or more recommended tank configurations returned by runEqualizationTankCalculation, each with a recommended volume and associated TankSeries information.
Allow the user to select a recommended configuration, with the primary recommendation preselected when available, and keep that selection in sync with URL parameters.

Provide a “View compatible tank series” action on each recommended configuration that navigates to equipment.html with categoryId, tankSeriesId, and minTankVolumeM3 URL parameters derived from the selected configuration.
Offer breadcrumb navigation back to the Tools & Calculators overview (tools.html) without using any additional interfaces or storage.
user_workflows: 1) Basic sizing and recommendation workflow: (a) User opens calculator_equalization_tank.html. (b) Header context is loaded via getSiteHeaderContext to update header icons. (c) User enters a flow rate value, selects a flow rate unit (from the allowed EqualizationTankCalculation.flow_rate_unit enumeration), enters a peak factor and buffer time in hours. (d) User submits the form, triggering runEqualizationTankCalculation with the current form values. (e) The page receives the EqualizationTankCalculation record and recommended_configurations array, stores them in page state, and updates the URL to reflect the current input parameters. (f) The calculation summary component displays required volume and inputs; the recommended configurations list displays options and automatically highlights the primary recommended configuration if one is indicated.

2) Selecting a configuration and going to compatible products: (a) After a calculation has run and recommendations are visible, the user reviews each configuration’s recommended volume and TankSeries details. (b) The user clicks on a specific configuration row/card to select it if they prefer a different option than the primary recommendation. (c) The selection event updates the local selection state and updates URL parameters (for example, selectedSeriesId and selectedVolumeM3) so the state can be restored on reload or shared. (d) The user clicks the “View compatible tank series” action on the selected configuration. (e) The page navigates to equipment.html with URL parameters categoryId set to the equalization tanks category slug, tankSeriesId set to the selected configuration’s TankSeries.id, and minTankVolumeM3 set to that configuration’s recommended_volume_m3. (f) The equipment listing page (outside this spec) uses those parameters to prefilter to compatible equalization tank products.

3) Returning to a calculation from a shared or bookmarked URL: (a) User or colleague opens calculator_equalization_tank.html with query parameters for flow_rate_value, flow_rate_unit, peak_factor, buffer_time_hours, and optionally an autoRun flag and a selectedSeriesId. (b) The calculator form component reads these URL parameters on initialization and prepopulates form fields. (c) If all required inputs are present and autoRun indicates an automatic run, the component immediately calls runEqualizationTankCalculation with those values. (d) Results and recommendations are displayed; if selectedSeriesId matches one of the returned configurations’ TankSeries.id values, that configuration is marked as selected. (e) The user can adjust inputs and recalculate; when they do, the URL parameters are updated to represent the new state.

4) Navigating back to Tools & Calculators: (a) User clicks the breadcrumb link labeled for Tools & Calculators. (b) The breadcrumb component routes the browser to tools.html with no additional parameters. (c) No calculation state from the calculator page is stored in any persistent client storage; if the user later returns via navigation or back button, any state that should persist must come from the URL parameters present at that time.
interactions: On page load, a header context loader component calls getSiteHeaderContext without parameters and uses the returned counts and totals to update the header icons (quote list, comparison, reading list, service inquiry).

On page load, the calculator form reads URL query parameters for flow_rate_value, flow_rate_unit, peak_factor, buffer_time_hours, a flag indicating whether to auto-run the calculation, and optionally selectedSeriesId and selectedVolumeM3. Valid values are applied to initial form state and selection state.

When the user edits a form field (flow rate value, flow rate unit, peak factor, buffer time), onChange handlers update the in-memory form state. The URL is not updated until the user submits to avoid polluting the URL with incomplete or invalid inputs.

When the user submits the calculator form, an onSubmit handler validates that all required fields are present and within allowed ranges. If valid, it calls runEqualizationTankCalculation with flow_rate_value, flow_rate_unit, peak_factor, and buffer_time_hours as provided by the user.

When runEqualizationTankCalculation resolves, the page updates its state with the returned EqualizationTankCalculation record (including calculated_required_volume_m3 and recommended_volume_m3) and the recommended_configurations array, and the URL query string is updated using history APIs to contain the current input values and a flag that indicates a calculation has been run.

When new calculation results are available, the calculation summary component’s onCalculationUpdate handler refreshes its display to show the key inputs and calculated required volume. If no calculation is present, the component remains hidden or shows a neutral placeholder state.

When recommended_configurations are present, the configurations list component renders each configuration with its recommended_volume_m3 and its associated TankSeries metadata. It determines the initially selected configuration either from the is_primary_recommendation flag or from a selectedSeriesId present in the URL, if the latter matches a returned TankSeries.id.

When the user clicks on a configuration item to select it, an onConfigurationSelect handler marks that configuration as selected, unselects others, and updates the URL parameters to include its TankSeries.id and recommended_volume_m3 so that selection can be reconstructed on reload.

When the user clicks the “View compatible tank series” button within a configuration item, an onViewCompatibleClick handler reads the selected configuration’s TankSeries.id and recommended_volume_m3 and navigates to equipment.html with URL parameters categoryId, tankSeriesId, and minTankVolumeM3 set from that configuration.
When the user clicks the breadcrumb link back to Tools & Calculators, an onBreadcrumbClick handler navigates to tools.html, using standard browser navigation without modifying any additional state.

If runEqualizationTankCalculation returns an empty recommended_configurations array or an error, the configurations list component responds by clearing its display and showing an appropriate system message; it does not fabricate any static configuration data.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page relies solely on URL query parameters and in-memory component state; no localStorage, sessionStorage, or other client-side persistence is used. On initial load, the following URL parameters are parsed: (1) flow_rate_value: numeric string, mapped directly to the calculator form's flow rate value; if this is absent or invalid, the form field remains empty. (2) flow_rate_unit: string constrained to the allowed EqualizationTankCalculation.flow_rate_unit enumeration; if invalid or missing, the unit selector falls back to its component-defined default. (3) peak_factor: numeric string for EqualizationTankCalculation.peak_factor; if invalid or missing, the corresponding field is left empty. (4) buffer_time_hours: numeric string for EqualizationTankCalculation.buffer_time_hours; if invalid or missing, the corresponding field is left empty. (5) autoRun (or equivalent flag name): if present and indicating true, and if all required numeric inputs and flow_rate_unit are valid, the form automatically calls runEqualizationTankCalculation with these values. (6) selectedSeriesId: string; after new calculation results are received, the configurations list checks whether any returned configuration has TankSeries.id equal to this value; if so, that configuration is marked selected; otherwise, selection falls back to the configuration where is_primary_recommendation is true (if such exists). (7) selectedVolumeM3 (or similar name): numeric string; used only as a hint to repopulate selection metadata in the URL; the authoritative volume for display always comes from the latest recommended_configurations data. After any successful form submission, the browser history is updated (via pushState or replaceState) so that the URL reflects the current input parameters (flow_rate_value, flow_rate_unit, peak_factor, buffer_time_hours) and indicates that a calculation has run (autoRun or similar flag). The URL does not store calculated_required_volume_m3 or other derived fields; these are always taken from the latest calculation record returned by runEqualizationTankCalculation. After any configuration selection, the URL is updated to include selectedSeriesId and a selectedVolumeM3 value taken from the selected configuration's recommended_volume_m3. Navigating away via breadcrumbs or the "View compatible tank series" action does not modify any data models; navigation is performed purely by changing window.location to the appropriate target URL with its query parameters. Specifically, the "View compatible tank series" action navigates to equipment.html with query parameters: categoryId set to the equalization tank category slug, tankSeriesId set to the selected configuration's TankSeries.id, and minTankVolumeM3 set to the selected configuration's recommended_volume_m3. Any reload of calculator_equalization_tank.html reconstructs form and selection state exclusively from the query parameters present at that time and from fresh calls to the allowed interfaces.

components.functionality: On initial render, this component calls getSiteHeaderContext to retrieve summary counts and totals for quote list items, comparison items, reading list items, and service inquiry items. It passes these values to the site header so the corresponding icons and badges display up-to-date counts and (for the quote list) total value and currency.

Renders a breadcrumb trail for navigation, with an interactive link back to the Tools & Calculators overview (tools.html) and a non-clickable label for the current Equalization Tank Sizing Calculator page. Clicking the Tools breadcrumb navigates to tools.html.

Manages the sizing input form for equalization tanks. It initializes its fields from URL parameters when available, validates user inputs, and on submit calls runEqualizationTankCalculation with flow_rate_value, flow_rate_unit, peak_factor, and buffer_time_hours. After a successful call, it updates page-level calculation state and synchronizes the URL parameters to represent the current input values and that a calculation has been performed.

Displays a concise summary of the latest equalization tank calculation. It shows the input parameters (flow rate, unit, peak factor, buffer time) and key output fields such as calculated_required_volume_m3 and recommended_volume_m3 from the EqualizationTankCalculation record returned by runEqualizationTankCalculation. If no calculation record is present, the component remains hidden or shows a neutral placeholder.

Receives the recommended_configurations array returned from runEqualizationTankCalculation alongside the EqualizationTankCalculation record and renders a list of recommended tank configurations. It determines which configuration is selected, either based on a primary recommendation flag or on a selectedSeriesId parsed from the URL. It propagates selection changes to the URL and exposes the currently selected configuration to child components, including enabling the appropriate "View compatible tank series" action.

Represents a single recommended tank configuration within the list. It displays the configuration's recommended_volume_m3 and associated TankSeries fields (such as name, series_code, and volume range). When clicked, it triggers selection. It also contains the "View compatible tank series" button, which, when activated, navigates to equipment.html with categoryId set to the equalization tanks category slug, tankSeriesId set to this configuration's TankSeries.id, and minTankVolumeM3 set to this configuration's recommended_volume_m3.

Coordinates reading and writing of URL query parameters with the calculator form and configuration selection. On initialization, it parses parameters for flow_rate_value, flow_rate_unit, peak_factor, buffer_time_hours, an auto-run indicator, selectedSeriesId, and selectedVolumeM3 and forwards them to the form and recommended-configurations-list components. After form submission and calculation completion, it updates the URL to include the current inputs and an indication that a calculation has run. After configuration selection, it updates the URL with the selectedSeriesId and selectedVolumeM3. It does not interact with any storage other than the URL and does not call any interfaces directly.

layout.grid_system: Implicit fixed-width grid, approximately 2-column layout within a centered 900–980px container. Not a modern 12-column system, but conceptually a main-content column (~70–75%) and a left sidebar (~25–30%) with occasional full-width header and navigation rows.
layout.layout_pattern: Use the classic boxed, fixed‑width layout: a full‑width patterned background with a centered, white page container (~940px wide). Inside, stack horizontal bands from top to bottom: (1) a header band handled by the site-header-context-loader (logo, banner, main navigation), (2) a narrow breadcrumb band, and (3) a two-column content band. Within the two-column band, the left column (~25–30% width) functions as a compact sidebar for the calculation-results-summary, while the wider right column (~70–75% width) contains the main functional content: the equalization-tank-calculator-form at the top and, directly beneath it, the recommended-configurations-list composed of repeated recommended-configuration-item rows. The equalization-calculator-url-state component is non-visual and does not occupy layout space.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use the classic boxed, fixed‑width layout: a full‑width patterned background with a centered, white page container (~940px wide). Inside, stack horizontal bands from top to bottom: (1) a header band handled by the site-header-context-loader (logo, banner, main navigation), (2) a narrow breadcrumb band, and (3) a two-column content band. Within the two-column band, the left column (~25–30% width) functions as a compact sidebar for the calculation-results-summary, while the wider right column (~70–75% width) contains the main functional content: the equalization-tank-calculator-form at the top and, directly beneath it, the recommended-configurations-list composed of repeated recommended-configuration-item rows. The equalization-calculator-url-state component is non-visual and does not occupy layout space.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Visually, the top header band (logo/banner/nav) occupies roughly 15–20% of the initial viewport height; the breadcrumb band beneath it is slim (about 5% of viewport height at most). The two-column content band fills the remaining 70–80% of the viewport. Inside this band, the left sidebar column uses about 1/4 of the container width for the calculation-results-summary box, and the right main column uses about 3/4 of the width for the calculator form and recommendations. Vertical gaps between major blocks (header, breadcrumb, main content, individual sections within the main column) follow a modest spacing of ~12–16px; tighter gaps (~8px) separate related elements like form rows and configuration items. Internal padding in boxes and cards remains compact at ~8–12px on all sides.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain a compact, information-dense feel: long form sections, closely stacked fields, and minimal whitespace between related elements. The calculator form uses small label–field spacing and tight vertical rhythm (4–8px between controls). The calculation-results-summary is a tight sidebar module with short labels and values packed closely. The recommended-configurations-list presents configuration items as narrow, full-width rows with modest padding and small gaps between them, avoiding large visual breathing spaces. Headings (for the form, results summary, and recommended list) are present but slim, using a blue header strip or bold blue text to separate dense blocks without introducing large vertical breaks.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Follow an F-pattern flow: the eye starts at the top left corporate logo inside the header, scans horizontally across the banner/nav, then drops down to the breadcrumb line. From there, attention is drawn diagonally to the bold blue heading of the Equalization Tank Sizing Calculator at the top of the right main column. The user’s primary reading path runs vertically down through the calculator form. Meanwhile, the left sidebar calculation-results-summary is caught peripherally as a dense, vertically aligned block of key numeric outputs, serving as a secondary reference. After completing or interacting with the form, attention continues down the right column into the recommended-configurations-list, scanning configuration items linearly from top to bottom. Any red highlight (e.g., for active states or key warnings) is used sparingly as a secondary attractor within this overall F-pattern.

layout.overall_description: The page sits inside a centered, white boxed container against a subtle patterned background. At the very top of this box, the site header stretches the full container width, combining a left-aligned company logo and name with a horizontal strip of industrial imagery and a navigation bar below, all in a conservative blue-and-white palette with small red accents. Directly under the header, a thin breadcrumb bar spans the content width, acting as a compact navigation aid. Beneath this, the page splits into a slightly asymmetrical two-column layout: a narrow left sidebar and a wider right main column. The left sidebar starts near the top of the content band with a boxed calculation-results-summary module, presented as a tight white card with a slim blue title bar and a stack of dense rows showing key tank sizing outputs. To its right, occupying most of the horizontal space, the Equalization Tank Sizing Calculator form appears as the primary content block, with a bold blue section heading and a dense vertical flow of labeled input fields, selectors, and action buttons. Immediately below the form, still within the right column, the recommended-configurations-list introduces another blue-topped section followed by a vertical series of narrow configuration-item rows, each a compact bar with a small thumbnail on the left and text details on the right. Vertical spacing between these blocks is modest, preserving the information-heavy, utilitarian feel of an older corporate site, while the consistent banding and two-column structure guide the eye smoothly from the top header down through calculation, summary, and recommendations.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component occupies the uppermost band inside the centered white page container, spanning 100% of the container width (approximately 940px) and about 15–20% of the initial viewport height. It is composed of two stacked rows: a taller top row for the company logo and any contextual imagery, and a shorter bottom row for the main horizontal navigation bar. The logo is positioned at the top-left corner of this band, with any banner image or contextual header content extending to the right, filling the remaining width. The navigation bar runs immediately below, as a full-width horizontal strip with menu items arranged left to right, each with modest horizontal padding (~8–12px) and minimal vertical padding (~4–6px). The entire header band sits flush with the top of the page container, with no extra margin above, and has a small margin (~12–16px) below before the breadcrumb bar begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block at the top of the page container; scrolls off-screen normally as the user scrolls down, with no fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb bar is positioned directly beneath the header band, spanning the full width of the inner content area of the white container. It is a slim horizontal strip, roughly 24–32px tall, with breadcrumbs left-aligned. A light grey or subtly beveled background differentiates it from the white content area, echoing the conservative corporate style. There is a modest top margin (~8px) from the bottom of the header band and a similar bottom margin (~12px) before the main two-column content band begins. Text inside is compact, with minimal vertical padding (~4px) and tight spacing between breadcrumb links, reinforcing the information-dense feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static horizontal band between header and main content that scrolls with the page; no sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The calculator form sits at the top of the right main column within the two-column content band. The two-column band begins just below the breadcrumb, with the main column occupying about 70–75% of the container width on the right and the sidebar occupying about 25–30% on the left. The form’s outer container is a white card-like block aligned to the right column’s left edge and extending to its right edge, giving it a rectangular footprint with modest internal padding (~12px) on all sides. At the top of the form block, a slim blue heading bar or bold blue text label reads 'Equalization Tank Sizing Calculator', visually echoing the blue section headings seen in the reference mockup. Below this heading, input fields are arranged in a single vertical stack or in simple two-column rows (label on the left, input on the right) but always constrained within this right-column width. Vertical spacing between individual input rows is tight (~8px), with label-to-control spacing as small as 4–6px, preserving compactness. Grouped sections within the form are separated with slightly larger gaps (~12–16px) or subtle rules, but the overall block remains dense. The primary Calculate/Update button row appears at the bottom of this form block, aligned to the right edge or centered within the block, with a small top margin (~8–12px) above it and a slightly larger bottom margin (~12–16px) separating the form from the recommended-configurations-list below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block anchored to the top of the right main column; it expands vertically as needed and scrolls with the content, without floating or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The calculation-results-summary occupies the top of the left sidebar column within the main two-column content band. This sidebar column starts at the same vertical position as the calculator form but sits against the left inner edge of the page container, taking about 25–30% of the container width. The summary is presented as a compact card with a slightly framed appearance: a thin border or subtle bevel, a pale background, and a slim blue title bar at the top labeled with something like 'Results Summary' to mirror the section-heading motif from the mockup. Inside, key output values (e.g., required equalization volume, recommended retention time) are displayed as a vertical list of short label–value rows, each row with tight vertical spacing (4–6px between rows) and minimal padding (~8px) around the content. The card’s top aligns horizontally with the top of the calculator form on the right, creating a clean horizontal starting line. Below this card, the rest of the sidebar may remain empty or reserved for future modules, but the sidebar width is visually maintained to reinforce the two-column structure.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static sidebar block pinned to the top of the left column; it scrolls with the page. It does not overlay or float over the main column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component is positioned directly below the equalization-tank-calculator-form in the right main column, keeping the same column width and alignment. There is a modest vertical gap (~12–16px) between the bottom of the form and the top of this list. At the top of the list, a blue section heading bar or bold blue text label (e.g., 'Recommended Configurations') spans the full width of the right column, mirroring the style of the form heading and the blue headings observed in the mockup’s reference sections. Below this heading, the recommended configuration items are stacked in a single vertical column, each item occupying the full width of the right column. The list itself forms a visually continuous block, with only narrow gaps (~8px) between configuration items and slightly larger padding (~8–12px) inside each item to maintain the utilitarian, dense layout. The overall list can extend well below the initial viewport, emphasizing vertical scrolling while preserving the fixed-width, boxed environment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static vertical stack of items within the right column; the list grows vertically and scrolls with the rest of the main content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each configuration item appears as a horizontally structured row within the recommended-configurations-list, spanning the full width of the right main column. The item is a low-profile card with a light border or subtle background tint to distinguish it from the page background, consistent with the skeuomorphic, beveled-box style. Internal padding is compact (~8–12px on all sides). Within the row, a small product or schematic thumbnail is positioned on the left, taking roughly 15–20% of the item’s width, with its height constrained so that the entire row remains relatively shallow. To the right of the thumbnail, occupying the remaining 80–85% of the width, textual information (configuration name in bold, key parameters, and a short description or tags) is stacked vertically with tight spacing (4–8px between lines). A call-to-action link or button (e.g., 'View compatible tanks') sits at the bottom-right of this text block, aligned to the item’s right edge. Vertical gaps between consecutive items in the list are small (~8px), so the stack reads as a dense list of options, consistent with the information-heavy aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a standard block-level row inside the list; no independent floating, fixed, or sticky behavior. Each item’s height adapts to its content but remains compact.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component has no visual representation on the page. It functions behind the scenes, synchronizing the calculator’s state with the URL. Conceptually, it is associated with the main content band (encompassing the form, results summary, and recommendations), but it does not occupy any visible space, does not affect spacing or alignment, and does not alter the fixed-width, two-column structure.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; no spatial footprint. It is logically mounted alongside the main page components but does not render any DOM elements that influence layout.

name: Contact
title: Contact
description: Contact page providing general contact details, a simple inquiry form, a link to the distributor finder, and regional support information for industrial wastewater treatment inquiries.

core_features: Load and display global header context (quote list, comparison, reading list, and service inquiry counts) using getSiteHeaderContext.
Fetch and display general company contact details (company name, phone, email, address) using getContactPageInfo.
Fetch and display regional support contact details using getContactPageInfo.regional_support.
Provide a general contact form bound to ContactRequest fields (name, email, phone, company, subject, message) and submit via submitContactRequest.
Pre-fill the contact form subject field based on the optional URL parameter ?subject=… when present.
Display submission feedback (success or failure) based on the submitContactRequest response while staying on the Contact page.
Provide a clear navigation control to open the Distributor Finder page (distributors.html).
Provide navigation back to the Home page (index.html) via breadcrumb.
user_workflows:
Workflow: View contact details and decide how to reach out
1) User opens contact.html optionally with a subject parameter in the URL (e.g., contact.html?subject=Quote%20follow-up).
2) On page load, the page calls getSiteHeaderContext to populate header icons and getContactPageInfo to obtain company and regional contact information.
3) The contact-info-panel component displays the general phone, email, and address from getContactPageInfo.
4) The regional-support-list component displays regional support entries from getContactPageInfo.regional_support.
5) User chooses whether to use the general contact details, a regional support contact, the contact form, or the Distributor Finder button based on their needs.

Workflow: Submit a general contact inquiry from the Contact form
1) User navigates to contact.html (optionally with ?subject= pre-set from quote_list.html, service_inquiry.html, equipment.html, or services.html).
2) The contact-form component reads the URL parameter subject; if present, it initializes the Subject field with that value.
3) User fills in required fields: Name, Email, and Message; optionally fills Phone, Company, and edits Subject.
4) User submits the form; client-side validation checks required fields are not empty and that Email has a valid format.
5) On successful validation, contact-form calls submitContactRequest with the current field values (name, email, phone, company, subject, message).
6) When submitContactRequest returns, the submission-feedback component reads { success, message, contact_request } from the response.
7) If success is true, the form can clear or lock fields and a success status message is shown; if success is false, an error status message is displayed and the user may correct the form and resubmit.

Workflow: Navigate to the Distributor Finder from the Contact page
1) User arrives on contact.html and sees a clearly labeled control (button or link) for finding a local distributor.
2) User clicks the 'Find a Distributor' control within the distributor-cta component.
3) The page triggers navigation to distributors.html without additional URL parameters (per outgoing_connections specification).
4) User continues distributor-related actions on distributors.html.

Workflow: Navigate back to Home using breadcrumbs
1) User is on contact.html and sees a breadcrumb path including Home and Contact.
2) User clicks the Home breadcrumb link within the breadcrumb-nav component.
3) The page navigates to index.html.
4) No contact-specific state is preserved or passed in the URL during this navigation.
interactions: On page load, call getSiteHeaderContext and use its return values to update header indicators (quote list count and total value, comparison count, reading list count, service inquiry count).
On page load, call getContactPageInfo and use its return values to populate company-level contact information and regional support listings.
On page initialization, parse the subject URL parameter (if provided) and set the initial value of the contact form's Subject field to that value.
User edits contact form fields (Name, Email, Phone, Company, Subject, Message); these values are stored in component state mapped to ContactRequest fields.
On contact form submission (onSubmit), validate required fields, then call submitContactRequest(name, email, phone, company, subject, message).
After submitContactRequest resolves, update submission-feedback state with success and message; optionally bind contact_request.id or status for further internal use (e.g., disabling re-submit).
Clicking the distributor-cta button/link triggers client-side navigation to distributors.html.
Clicking the Home item in breadcrumb-nav triggers navigation to index.html.

Clicking general phone or regional support phone values may initiate a call via a tel: link; clicking general or regional emails may open a mail client via a mailto: link (purely navigational interactions using data from getContactPageInfo).
page_functionality.state_logic: The Contact page state is driven primarily by the URL parameter subject and the responses from the assigned interfaces. On initial load of contact.html, the page parses window.location.search for the subject parameter. If subject is present and is a non-empty string, the contact-form component sets its internal Subject field state to that value. If subject is not present, the Subject field initializes as an empty string and can be freely edited by the user. The subject URL parameter is read-only; the page does not modify URL parameters when the user edits the Subject field. Regardless of URL parameters, getSiteHeaderContext and getContactPageInfo are called once on initial render (or when the page is reloaded) to populate header context, general contact info, and regional support lists. The response from submitContactRequest does not change the URL; instead, it updates in-page state in submission-feedback (success flag and message string). A successful submission may optionally reset the contact-form field states (e.g., empty strings) or leave them intact, but this reset is handled entirely in component state and not reflected in the URL. Navigation to distributors.html and index.html is performed via standard link navigation without additional query parameters from this page, as specified in outgoing_connections.

components.functionality: On page load, retrieves header summary information using getSiteHeaderContext and provides it to the global header component so it can display counts and totals for quote list, comparison items, reading list, and service inquiry items.

Displays a functional breadcrumb trail for navigation, including a link back to the Home page (index.html) and the current Contact page label (non-clickable). Clicking Home navigates the user back to the site homepage.

Displays general company contact details including company name, main phone, general email, and physical mailing/office address. Data is loaded from getContactPageInfo. Phone and email values are rendered as actionable links (e.g., tel:, mailto:) using the returned dynamic values.

Lists regional or global support contacts based on the regional_support array returned by getContactPageInfo. For each entry, displays region, support email, and support phone, enabling users to contact the appropriate regional office or representative.

Provides a general inquiry form for industrial wastewater treatment questions. The form includes fields mapped to ContactRequest: name (required), email (required), phone (optional), company (optional), subject (optional, but pre-filled from URL parameter if provided), and message (required). On submission, validates required fields and then calls submitContactRequest with the current field values.

Receives the response from submitContactRequest and displays a status message to the user. If success is true, shows a success confirmation; if false, shows an error notification using the message returned by the interface. May also store contact_request.id or status to prevent duplicate submissions or to display a reference ID.
Provides a prominent navigation control labeled for finding a distributor. When clicked, navigates the user from contact.html to distributors.html without passing additional parameters.

layout.grid_system: Implicit fixed-width grid, approximately 2-column layout within a centered 900–980px container. Not a modern 12-column system, but conceptually a main-content column (~70–75%) and a left sidebar (~25–30%) with occasional full-width header and navigation rows.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the classic boxed corporate layout seen in the mockup: a full-width patterned background with a centered, fixed-width page container (≈940–960px). Above this layout (not part of the component list) are the logo/banner strip and the full-width main navigation. Directly beneath navigation, a thin full-width breadcrumb strip introduces the content area. Below that, the interior splits into two columns: a narrower left sidebar (≈25–30% width) hosting stacked, compact information blocks (general contact info and a distributor call-to-action), and a wider right column (≈70–75% width) containing the primary 'Contact' page heading, feedback status, the main inquiry form, and a vertically flowing regional support list. All components stack in a linear, top-to-bottom sequence within their respective columns, mirroring the mockup's utilitarian, content-first arrangement.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the classic boxed corporate layout seen in the mockup: a full-width patterned background with a centered, fixed-width page container (≈940–960px). Above this layout (not part of the component list) are the logo/banner strip and the full-width main navigation. Directly beneath navigation, a thin full-width breadcrumb strip introduces the content area. Below that, the interior splits into two columns: a narrower left sidebar (≈25–30% width) hosting stacked, compact information blocks (general contact info and a distributor call-to-action), and a wider right column (≈70–75% width) containing the primary 'Contact' page heading, feedback status, the main inquiry form, and a vertically flowing regional support list. All components stack in a linear, top-to-bottom sequence within their respective columns, mirroring the mockup's utilitarian, content-first arrangement.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header/logo and imagery band plus navigation bar take the top portion of the viewport (≈20–25% combined), with the remaining vertical space devoted to the two-column content band. Within the content band, the breadcrumb occupies a slim strip across the full container width (≈5% of content height). Below it, roughly 70–75% of the horizontal width belongs to the main column on the right and 25–30% to the sidebar on the left. Vertically in the main column, the header-context occupies a shallow area at the top (≈10% of the content column height), followed immediately by a low-profile submission-feedback banner when present (≈5%), then the contact form as the dominant vertical block (≈35–45%), and finally the regional-support-list extending downwards (≈30–40% depending on content). In the sidebar, the contact-info-panel consumes the upper two-thirds or so of the column, with the distributor-cta as a shorter, compact module near the bottom. Vertical spacing between modules is modest (≈8–16px) with internal padding around 8–12px, preserving the tight, information-dense character.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content remains compact and utilitarian. Text blocks are relatively dense, with modest line spacing and small vertical gaps between headings and paragraphs (≈4–8px). Panels such as the contact-info-panel and regional-support-list use narrow internal padding (≈8–12px) and limited whitespace, emphasizing information over breathing room. The contact form fields are closely stacked with minimal vertical gaps (≈8px) and concise labels, reflecting the mockup’s emphasis on fitting many elements within a limited height. Breadcrumb, status banner, and button areas feel functional and slightly compressed rather than airy. Overall, whitespace is present but controlled; the layout favors clear grouping and alignment rather than spaciousness.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows an F-pattern akin to the mockup. The eye first travels across the (external) logo/banner, then the full-width navigation bar. It then drops immediately to the breadcrumb strip, scanning left to right. From there, attention moves diagonally down into the right-hand main column, landing on the 'Contact' header-context as the primary entry point. Directly below that, any submission-feedback banner is noticed as a thin colored strip, then the user’s gaze proceeds down through the stacked form fields. While reading or filling the form, the left sidebar remains in peripheral vision; the contact-info-panel at the top-left is a secondary, stable reference block, and the red-accented distributor-cta at the bottom-left acts as a tertiary attractor similar to the red highlights in the mockup’s navigation and sidebar. After the form, the eye continues vertically into the regional-support-list, which uses repeated heading lines and dense text blocks—mirroring the reference-entry pattern in the mockup—to guide scanning down the page.

layout.overall_description: Inside a centered, mid-width white page box set against a subtle patterned background, the content begins with a slim breadcrumb bar stretching across the full container just under the main navigation. Below this strip, the page splits into two clearly defined columns. On the left, a relatively narrow sidebar runs from near the top of the content area toward the footer, filled with compact, boxed information modules: at the top the general contact information card, and beneath it a smaller panel emphasizing the distributor finder call-to-action. On the right, a wider main column starts with the page’s 'Contact' heading and intro context, set just below the breadcrumb. Immediately under this sits a low, full-width status banner that appears when a form submission succeeds or fails. The main inquiry form then occupies the bulk of the right column’s upper area, stacking fields tightly but clearly in a vertical sequence. As the user scrolls down, this main column continues into the regional support information, which is organized in repeated blocks: each region begins with a small blue heading bar and possibly an icon, followed by compact lines of contacts and details. The overall effect is that of a traditional corporate contact page from an earlier web era—boxy, structured, and slightly skeuomorphic—with a strong right-column focus for interactive tasks and a secondary left column always available for quick reference and alternative navigation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The header-context sits in the right-hand main content column at the very top of the two-column band, directly beneath the full-width breadcrumb strip. It aligns flush with the right column’s left edge and spans the full width of that column (≈70–75% of the page container). Vertically, it is a relatively shallow panel: the main 'Contact' page title appears on a single line or two at most, left-aligned, with a modest amount of space above and below (≈8–12px padding inside the panel, and ≈8px margin to the breadcrumb above and to the status banner below). The style is consistent with the mockup’s primary headings: a prominent blue title, possibly with a thin horizontal rule or lightly beveled top edge to echo the skeuomorphic bar treatment. Any short descriptive text sits just below the title with minimal vertical separation (≈4–6px), maintaining the page’s information-dense feel. This header-context does not span the entire page width; it is visually anchored to the main column to reinforce that this area is where the primary reading and interaction occur.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element within the main column; it scrolls normally with the rest of the content and defines the top of the right-column content stack.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb navigation forms a thin, full-width horizontal band immediately below the global navigation bar and above the two-column content area. It spans the entire width of the centered page container (≈940–960px) from left to right, creating a clear separation between the global chrome (logo/navigation) and the page-specific content. The breadcrumb text is left-aligned, starting near the container’s left edge with modest horizontal padding (≈12–16px) and minimal vertical height (≈24–28px total). The band itself feels compact and utilitarian: a light background tint or subtle gradient, a thin top or bottom border, and tightly spaced breadcrumb links. There is very little vertical space between this strip and the content below (≈8px), so that the header-context in the right column appears closely connected. The two columns (sidebar and main) visually begin immediately under this breadcrumb strip, with the breadcrumb not belonging explicitly to either column but acting as a full-width row across both.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width row inside the page container; it acts as a horizontal divider between navigation and content but does not stick or float.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-info-panel is positioned in the top portion of the left sidebar column, starting just below the breadcrumb strip and aligned with the left inner edge of the page container. It occupies the full width of this sidebar column (≈25–30% of the overall content width) and is presented as a compact white card on the same boxed background as the main content. The panel features a narrow header bar at its top—likely a blue beveled strip with the label 'Contact Information' or similar—to echo the mockup’s sidebar modules. Inside, company address, phone numbers, email, and possibly office hours are arranged in tightly stacked lines with modest internal padding (≈8–12px on all sides) and minimal spacing between lines (≈4px). Individual items may be separated by thin horizontal rules or small vertical gaps (≈4–6px), but overall the card remains dense and moderately tall, filling roughly the upper one-half to two-thirds of the visible sidebar height depending on content. There is a small margin (≈8–12px) below this panel before the next sidebar block (the distributor-cta) starts, creating a clear but compact separation between sidebar modules.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, top-aligned block in the left sidebar; it maintains its relative position as the first sidebar module and scrolls with the page content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The regional-support-list resides in the right-hand main column, directly beneath the contact form and spanning the full width of that column. It begins with a small vertical gap (≈12–16px) below the bottom of the form’s last element, then introduces a section title such as 'Regional Support' styled similarly to content section headings in the mockup: a blue heading line or a narrow colored bar with an icon at the left, followed by the title text. Below this, each region appears as a repeated block: a subheading line with the region name (e.g., 'Europe', 'Asia-Pacific') in bold blue text, possibly accompanied by a small icon, followed by dense paragraphs or line-separated contact details (names, phone numbers, emails) for that region. The text is packed closely with small vertical spacing between lines (≈4–6px) and between regions (≈8–12px), resembling the mockup’s reference entries. The overall list is fairly tall and scrollable, extending down the page as needed. It is visually contained within the main column boundaries, with no large margins on the left or right beyond the standard column padding (≈12–16px), reinforcing the information-heavy, document-like appearance of the main content area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static vertical content section within the main column; it extends downward as content grows, forming the lower part of the primary reading flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-form is the dominant interactive block in the right-hand main column, placed immediately below the header-context (and submission-feedback banner when present). It spans the full width of the main column, from its left inner edge to its right inner edge (≈70–75% of the container), with modest internal padding (≈12px) and a light border or subtle panel treatment that matches the utilitarian card style seen in the mockup. Form fields are arranged in a single vertical stack: labels are left-aligned above or to the left of inputs with minimal vertical separation (≈4px), and each field row is separated by a compact gap (≈8px). Multi-line elements such as message textareas occupy the full width of the form, while grouped fields (e.g., first/last name) may sit in a simple two-column sub-grid within this panel but still feel tightly packed. The submit button sits at the bottom-right of the form panel or left-aligned depending on the site’s overall pattern, but with only a small margin above it (≈8–12px). Overall, the form is fairly tall and dense, occupying a substantial portion of the visible right column height and seating directly above the regional-support-list with a small vertical gap (≈12–16px) between them. There is little extraneous whitespace, emphasizing clarity and task completion.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static central interaction block; it scrolls normally but acts as the main focus of the right column’s upper section.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The submission-feedback component appears as a low, full-width status banner within the right-hand main column, directly below the header-context and directly above the contact-form. When visible, it stretches across the entire main column width with a modest height (≈32–40px), housing short success or error messages in a single line or two. The banner uses a slightly stronger background color (e.g., muted green or red) with white or dark text, similar in visual weight to the red-highlighted active navigation item in the mockup, making it noticeable but not oversized. Vertical spacing is tight: there is a small gap (≈4–8px) between the header-context and the top of the banner, and another small gap of similar size between the bottom of the banner and the top edge of the contact-form. The banner’s left edge aligns with the left edge of the main column content, ensuring it feels like part of the form stack rather than a separate global message. When not active, this slot collapses entirely, with the contact-form moving up to sit just under the header-context.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally rendered static element within the main column; it does not stick or float but appears inline above the form when there is feedback to show.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The distributor-cta is placed in the left sidebar column directly beneath the contact-info-panel, forming a separate, compact module with a small vertical gap (≈8–12px) between them. It occupies the full width of the sidebar, but is relatively short in height—essentially a small card or bar containing a headline such as 'Find a Distributor' and a prominent call-to-action button or link. To mirror the mockup’s use of red as a secondary attractor, the CTA itself (button or link) is styled with a red or accent color and is centered or left-aligned within the card. Internal padding is modest (≈8–12px all around), keeping the module compact. The card may have a lightly beveled or gradient header or border, consistent with the skeuomorphic feel of other sidebar elements. As the user scans the page in an F-pattern, this CTA sits in their peripheral vision while they work in the main column, offering an alternative path (to the distributor finder) without competing visually with the main contact form.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static sidebar module; it stays anchored below the contact-info-panel and scrolls with the rest of the page content.

name: Distributor Finder
title: Distributor Finder
description: Page to search for industrial wastewater treatment distributors by ZIP/postal code, radius, and market segment, and navigate to distributor detail pages.

core_features: Initialize header icon counts and totals by calling getSiteHeaderContext when the page loads.
Provide a distributor search form with fields for ZIP/postal code, search radius, and market segment, prefilled from URL parameters when present.
Trigger distributor search requests via searchDistributors using the current form values (ZIP/postal code, radius, market segment).
Display search results from searchDistributors as a list of distributors ordered by distance, showing distributor name, city, distance, and supported markets.
Allow users to click a distributor name in the results to navigate to distributor_detail.html with the corresponding distributorId URL parameter.
Synchronize search state with URL parameters (zip, radiusKm, marketSegment) so searches can be bookmarked, shared, and restored on reload or back/forward navigation.
Handle empty-result and error cases for distributor searches with appropriate feedback and the ability to adjust search criteria and resubmit.
user_workflows:
Workflow: New search from footer link (no prefilled parameters)
1. User opens distributors.html from the footer link without URL parameters.
2. The page loads and calls getSiteHeaderContext to populate header icon counts.
3. The Distributor Search Initializer reads URL parameters (none present), leaving the search form fields empty or using implementation-defined defaults.
4. User enters a ZIP/postal code, a radius value, and selects a market segment in the search form.
5. User submits the form; the URL-state component updates the URL parameters (zip, radiusKm, marketSegment) to reflect the entered values.
6. The search form submission triggers a call to searchDistributors with the current form values.
7. When searchDistributors returns, the results list component displays distributors ordered by distance with key summary information.
8. User clicks a distributor name in the results, and the browser navigates to distributor_detail.html with distributorId set to the selected distributor's id.

Workflow: Prefilled search from Contact page
1. User clicks a 'Find a Distributor' link or button on contact.html which navigates to distributors.html with zip (and optionally radiusKm and/or marketSegment) URL parameters.
2. On page load, getSiteHeaderContext is called to update header context.
3. Distributor Search Initializer parses URL parameters and pre-populates the search form fields with zip, radiusKm, and marketSegment values from the URL.
4. If all required parameters for searchDistributors are present and valid, the initializer automatically triggers a searchDistributors call using those values.
5. The results list component displays the returned distributors; the user may adjust radius or market segment and resubmit.
6. Each resubmission updates the URL parameters and triggers a new searchDistributors call, refreshing the results list.

Workflow: Returning to a bookmarked search or using browser navigation
1. User previously performed a search on distributors.html; the URL now contains zip, radiusKm, and marketSegment parameters.
2. The user bookmarks this URL or shares it with a colleague.
3. When the bookmarked/shared URL is opened, the page loads, calls getSiteHeaderContext, and the initializer reads the URL parameters.
4. The search form is pre-filled from the URL parameters, and if valid, an automatic searchDistributors call is made to restore the same result set.
5. If the user modifies the search and submits again, the URL parameters are updated. Using the browser back/forward buttons changes the URL, and the initializer detects these changes, re-populates the form, and re-runs searchDistributors so that the displayed results always match the URL state.
interactions:
Interaction: Page load
- Trigger: Browser loads distributors.html.
- Behavior: Call getSiteHeaderContext to retrieve header counts and totals. Parse URL parameters (zip, radiusKm, marketSegment). Initialize the search form fields with these values if present. If the parameters are sufficient and valid for a search, automatically call searchDistributors with zip_postal_code=zip, radius_km=radiusKm, market_segment=marketSegment.

Interaction: User edits ZIP/postal code field
- Trigger: onChange in ZIP/postal code input.
- Behavior: Update the search form's internal state for the ZIP/postal code. Do not call searchDistributors yet. Mark the form as 'dirty' compared to current URL state so that on submit the URL will be updated.

Interaction: User edits radius field
- Trigger: onChange in radius input.
- Behavior: Update the form state for radius. Do not immediately call searchDistributors. Prepare to update radiusKm in the URL upon submit.

Interaction: User selects market segment
- Trigger: onChange in market segment selector.
- Behavior: Update the form state for market segment with a value that matches the DistributorSearch.market_segment enum. No immediate searchDistributors call; changes will be applied on form submit or explicit search action.

Interaction: User submits the search form
- Trigger: onSubmit event on the search form (e.g., clicking a 'Search' button or pressing Enter).
- Behavior: Validate that ZIP/postal code, radius, and market segment are present and valid. If validation passes, update the URL query parameters zip, radiusKm, and marketSegment to match the form state (using a URL-state manager that preserves other unrelated parameters). After updating the URL, call searchDistributors with zip_postal_code, radius_km, and market_segment taken from the form (or equivalently, from the updated URL). While waiting for results, the results list component can display a loading state.

Interaction: Handling searchDistributors response
- Trigger: Successful response from searchDistributors.
- Behavior: Bind the returned DistributorSearch object to the 'current search' state and bind the results array (each with distributor and distance_km) to the results list component. Clear any previous error state. If the results array is empty, the results list component displays an implementation-defined 'no results' message and keeps the form values unchanged so the user can adjust criteria.

Interaction: Handling searchDistributors errors
- Trigger: Error when calling searchDistributors (e.g., network error or validation failure returned from backend).
- Behavior: Set an error state in the page; the results list component displays an implementation-defined error message and does not display stale results. The search form remains populated so the user can correct input and resubmit. No URL parameter changes occur beyond those already applied on submit.

Interaction: Clicking a distributor name in results
- Trigger: onClick on a distributor name link in the results list.
- Behavior: Read the associated distributor.id from the bound Distributor object and navigate to distributor_detail.html with the URL parameter distributorId set to that value. No additional API calls from this page are required for navigation.

Interaction: Browser back/forward navigation
- Trigger: User clicks browser Back or Forward while on distributors.html, changing the URL parameters.
- Behavior: The URL-state manager detects parameter changes for zip, radiusKm, and marketSegment. It updates the search form fields to match the new URL parameter values. If the new URL state contains a valid combination for searchDistributors, it triggers a new searchDistributors call so the results reflect the current URL.

Interaction: Manual URL editing and reload
- Trigger: User manually edits zip, radiusKm, or marketSegment parameters in the browser address bar and reloads the page.
- Behavior: On reload, the initializer reads the edited parameters, pre-fills the form, and if valid, triggers searchDistributors with those values, ensuring the view matches the URL.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page uses URL query parameters zip, radiusKm, and marketSegment as the single source of truth for search state. On initial load, the Distributor Search Initializer reads these parameters. If zip is present, it populates the ZIP/postal code form field with that value; otherwise the field is left empty or uses an implementation-defined default. If radiusKm is present and valid, it populates the radius field; otherwise it is left empty or uses an implementation-defined default. If marketSegment is present and matches an allowed DistributorSearch.market_segment enum value, the market segment selector is set accordingly; otherwise it uses an implementation-defined default market segment or remains unselected until the user chooses one. When all required fields are available and valid after parsing the URL, the initializer automatically calls searchDistributors using zip_postal_code=zip, radius_km=radiusKm, and market_segment=marketSegment. When the user submits the search form, the URL is updated to reflect the form state by setting or replacing zip, radiusKm, and marketSegment while preserving unrelated parameters. This URL update allows the search to be shareable and restorable. The searchDistributors call is always made with values that are in sync with the URL, either from the form (immediately before updating) or from the URL after it has been updated. The current DistributorSearch record and results array returned from searchDistributors are held in in-memory page state and are not persisted beyond the session. Browser back/forward navigation changes the URL; the page listens for these changes, re-parses zip, radiusKm, and marketSegment, updates the form, and optionally re-calls searchDistributors so that the displayed results always match the active URL. No state is stored in localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies; only URL parameters and in-memory state are used.

components.functionality: On page initialization, retrieve header context data (quote list, comparison, reading list, service inquiry counts and totals) using getSiteHeaderContext and bind the returned values to header UI elements.

On page load and on URL change, parse the URL parameters zip, radiusKm, and marketSegment to initialize or update the search form state. When all required parameters for a distributor search are present and valid, automatically trigger a searchDistributors call.

Provide input fields for ZIP/postal code, radius, and market segment. Maintain internal form state, validate required fields, and, on submit, update URL parameters to reflect the current form state and trigger a distributor search via searchDistributors.

Synchronize form state with URL parameters and vice versa. When the form is submitted, update zip, radiusKm, and marketSegment in the URL query string. When URL changes are detected (including back/forward navigation), notify the initializer and form to update their state accordingly.

Display the list of distributors returned by searchDistributors, ordered by distance. For each result, show distributor name, city, distance_km, and supported_markets summary. Handle and display loading, no-results, and error states. Provide navigation to distributor_detail.html when a distributor is selected.

Abstract calls to searchDistributors. Receive validated search criteria from the form or initializer (ZIP/postal code, radius, market segment), call searchDistributors with zip_postal_code, radius_km, and market_segment, then expose the resulting DistributorSearch and results array to the results list component while managing loading and error states.

layout.grid_system: Implicit fixed-width grid, approximately 2-column layout within a centered 900–980px container. Not a modern 12-column system, but conceptually a main-content column (~70–75%) and a left sidebar (~25–30%) with occasional full-width header and navigation rows.
layout.layout_pattern: The page sits on a full-width patterned background with a centered, fixed-width white container (~940–960px). Inside this box, bands stack from top to bottom: a full-width header strip (logo and possibly banner imagery), a full-width horizontal navigation bar, then a two-column content band. The left column is the site-wide sidebar (not defined as components here), the right column is the main content column. Within the right/main column, the Distributor Finder experience is stacked vertically: page heading (provided by the header context), then the distributor-search-form as a compact functional panel, followed immediately by the distributor-results-list as a long scrolling list. Non-visual components (initializers, service connector, URL sync) are treated as invisible layers backing these visible sections.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page sits on a full-width patterned background with a centered, fixed-width white container (~940–960px). Inside this box, bands stack from top to bottom: a full-width header strip (logo and possibly banner imagery), a full-width horizontal navigation bar, then a two-column content band. The left column is the site-wide sidebar (not defined as components here), the right column is the main content column. Within the right/main column, the Distributor Finder experience is stacked vertically: page heading (provided by the header context), then the distributor-search-form as a compact functional panel, followed immediately by the distributor-results-list as a long scrolling list. Non-visual components (initializers, service connector, URL sync) are treated as invisible layers backing these visible sections.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Roughly 15–20% of the initial viewport height is used by the header/logo band, another 5–8% by the navigation bar. The remaining ~75% of the viewport height is the two-column content region. Within the content region, the left sidebar occupies about 25–30% of the container width, and the main/right column about 70–75%. The distributor-search-form sits at the top of the right column, occupying its full width; the distributor-results-list consumes the rest of the right column, extending downward as needed. Vertical gaps between stacked modules in the right column (heading, search form, results list) are modest, around 12–16px. Inside the search form and individual results, paddings are compact (8–12px) in line with the conservative, information-dense style.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content is compact and utilitarian. Text blocks and form fields use a tight vertical rhythm with 4–8px spacing between related elements and 12–16px between distinct groups. The search form is dense, with labels, inputs, and the search button grouped closely in one compact card-like area. The results list displays multiple distributors per page with minimal extra whitespace: each result has a clear heading and a small contact/info block but limited breathing room between entries, echoing the long paragraphs and tightly packed references from the mockup.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye follows a classic F-pattern. It first catches the company logo at the top-left of the header, then scans horizontally across the header band. It moves down to the navigation bar, scanning left-to-right, where the 'Distributor Finder' item is highlighted in red as the active section. From there, attention drops diagonally into the main content’s top-left, landing on the main page heading and immediately on the distributor-search-form. The search inputs and action button form a horizontal scanning line in the right column. Once the search is made (or defaults displayed), reading proceeds vertically down through the distributor-results-list, with each result’s blue heading line acting like the blue reference headings in the mockup, creating repeatable anchor points as the user scrolls.

layout.overall_description: Visually, the Distributor Finder page feels like a classic, boxed corporate site from an earlier web era. A patterned background fills the browser, while a centered white rectangle around 940–960px wide holds all content. At the very top of this box, a medium-height header band spans the full width, with the company logo and name on the left and possibly a conservative industrial image extending to the right. Immediately below is a beveled, gradient-tinted navigation bar that stretches edge-to-edge within the white box; its active item for this page, ‘Distributor Finder,’ is called out in red, while other links sit in blue or neutral tones. Beneath the nav, the main content band is split into two columns: a narrower left sidebar and a wider right content area. The left side continues the site’s pattern of stacked, compact modules (such as global search, company motto, or news), visually grounding the layout but not directly part of the Distributor Finder components. The right column, occupying about three-quarters of the width, begins with the page heading and then a dense, panel-like search form framed by thin borders and subtle gradients. Immediately below, without large gaps, the results list extends downward in a single vertical column: each distributor appears as a compact block with a blue heading bar and small icon, followed by tightly spaced contact details and service information. The cumulative effect is a structured, utilitarian search-and-results interface that fits seamlessly into an old-generation corporate shell—information-heavy, restrained, and conservative, yet clear in hierarchy and flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component occupies the very top band inside the fixed-width white page container and orchestrates the header and navigation context for the Distributor Finder page. Visually, it corresponds to two stacked full-width strips within the container: first, a header/logo strip that spans the entire ~940–960px width, about 15–20% of the initial viewport height, with the company logo and name anchored at the top-left, some horizontal padding (12–16px) from the container edge, and optional industrial imagery extending to the right. Directly beneath sits the main navigation bar, a thinner strip (~5–8% of viewport height), also full-width within the container. Navigation items are arranged horizontally from left to right, each with generous horizontal padding (8–12px) but tight vertical padding (4–6px), set against a slightly beveled or gradient background. The active navigation item for this page, such as ‘Distributor Finder,’ is marked with red text or an underline to stand out within the blue-dominant palette. This entire header-and-nav block sits above the two-column content band, with a modest vertical gap (~12px) separating the nav bar from the start of the content area. It scrolls away with the page rather than being sticky, maintaining the classic, non-fixed header behavior from the original mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block at the top of the page container; participates in normal document flow, not fixed or sticky. It defines the top boundary of the content area and spans the full width of the central boxed layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component acts as a non-visual layer that initializes the distributor search state before any UI appears. Spatially, it lives logically at the top of the main content band, aligned with the right/main column but does not render visible elements. Think of it as occupying a zero-height slot immediately below the navigation bar and above the distributor-search-form in the stacking order, without adding any margin or padding. It ensures that when the user first sees the page, the search form and results area are already synchronized to default or URL-derived values, but the page’s visual layout—the header, the two-column structure, and the content spacing—remains unaffected.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual and non-intrusive; occupies no visual space and does not alter the flow or spacing of other components. It is conceptually positioned near the top of the main content column for initialization purposes only.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search form is the first visible functional element in the right/main column, appearing directly below the navigation bar and any page heading text. It is contained within the main column that occupies about 70–75% of the white container’s width, positioned to the right of the left sidebar with a consistent gutter (~16–24px) between columns. The form itself is presented as a compact, card-like panel: a subtle bordered rectangle with a very light blue or gray background tint, spanning roughly 95–100% of the main column width, leaving a narrow inner margin (8–12px) from the column’s edges. At the top-left of this panel, a bold blue title like ‘Find a Distributor’ echoes the blue reference headings from the mockup, paired with a small icon to the left, drawing the eye into the right column. Below the title, form controls are arranged in a dense, two-row layout. The first row runs left to right across most of the panel: a ZIP/postal code input aligned to the left, the radius selector immediately to its right, and, toward the right edge of the panel, the primary ‘Search’ button in red or a red-accented style for emphasis. These fields align to a baseline with minimal vertical padding (4–6px) above and below. The second row, directly beneath with about 8px vertical spacing, holds the market segment selector spanning roughly half to two-thirds of the panel width, with any additional filters placed to its right if available. Internal paddings around the edge of the panel are in the 8–12px range, giving just enough breathing room without breaking the dense, utilitarian feel. The panel has about 12–16px of vertical margin separating it from the page heading above and from the distributor-results-list below, so the page reads as a tight stack of functional sections in the right column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block within the main content column, occupying normal flow. It stretches across the width of the main column but does not overlap the left sidebar. It pushes the results list downward as its own height grows (e.g., on smaller screens when fields wrap).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component is entirely behind-the-scenes, synchronizing the search state with the URL. In layout terms, it sits conceptually near the distributor-search-form and distributor-results-list, but it does not render any visual UI. It occupies zero height and zero width, adding no spacing or visible markers. Its presence ensures that when users share or revisit the URL, the search form and results list appear in the correct state, yet the visual structure—header, nav, two-column layout, search panel, and results stack—remains unaffected and clean.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; occupies no space and does not participate in layout. It functions purely as a logical behavior attached to the search form and results list within the main column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The results list occupies the main content area immediately below the distributor-search-form within the right column. It spans the same width as the search panel—roughly 95–100% of the main column width—aligning flush with the form’s left edge and right edge to maintain a continuous vertical column of content. A modest vertical gap of about 12–16px separates the form from the first result. The list then extends downward, potentially beyond the viewport, forming a single scrollable column of distributor entries. Each result is styled analogous to the reference entries in the mockup: at the top of each item, a horizontal bar-like heading features the distributor’s name in bold blue text with a small icon to the left (e.g., a location pin or building silhouette). This heading line may have a subtle gradient or beveled effect, visually separating each result from the next. Directly under the heading, in a compact block with 8–12px internal padding, the distributor’s details are laid out: address lines, phone number, website link, and a short descriptor (markets served, key capabilities), all set in tight leading with 4–6px vertical spacing between lines. Related data (e.g., service area tags or a small “View details” link) is grouped at the bottom of each block with about 4–8px spacing from the last line of contact info. Between results, separation is minimal: either a thin horizontal rule or a soft background change with 8–12px vertical spacing, maintaining density but preserving scannability. The list consumes all remaining vertical space in the right column and scrolls with the page, while the left sidebar continues down alongside it, reinforcing the two-column F-pattern.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, vertically expanding block within the main content column. It follows the search form in normal document flow, growing in height with the number of results. It does not use sticky or floating behavior; scrolling is handled by the page as a whole.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component operates as the data conduit between the UI and the backend distributor search service. It has no visual footprint: users never see it as a discrete block, and it adds no margins, padding, or lines to the page. Conceptually, it is bound to the distributor-search-form (for triggering searches) and the distributor-results-list (for feeding data), but visually the page remains a clean stack of header, nav, two-column layout, search panel, and results list. Its presence should not shift or resize any visible elements; when it loads or updates data, only the content within the existing boundaries of the results list changes, maintaining the established layout and density.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; occupies no layout space. It behaves as a background service wired into the search form and results list, without altering structural flow or positioning.

name: Distributor Detail
title: Distributor Detail
description: Displays detailed information for a selected distributor, allows users to reveal contact details, initiate contact, and navigate back to the distributor search results or to the manufacturer contact page.

core_features: Load and display full details for the distributor specified by the distributorId URL parameter using getDistributorDetails.
Load header context (quote list, comparison, reading list, service inquiry counts and totals) using getSiteHeaderContext to keep global header icons accurate.
Show core distributor information including name, address, country, region served, and supported market segments.
